label: 16 Shared-Client #423
info:
BUGFIX DEMO: shared Modbus-Client — disable one Flex-Getter (#423)

FEATURES IN THIS FLOW
- Two Modbus-Flex-Getter nodes share ONE Modbus-Client
- Keeper Flex-Getter keeps polling while you disable the other
- Shows fix for GitHub #423 / #487 (disable one must not STOP siblings)

WHAT THE CONFIG DOES
- Both Flex-Getters → same Client (tcpPort 10517)
- showStatusActivities=true → you see connected / closed on the node
- Keeper inject repeats every 2 s → continuous SEE: debug traffic
- DISABLE ME inject is manual only (for optional compare)

HOW TO RUN
1) Deploy — wait until both Flex-Getters look active/connected
2) Watch Debug SEE: KEEP — payload arrays every ~2 s
3) Select node «DISABLE ME (#423)» → Disable → Deploy (should finish quickly, not ~15 s)
4) PASS: SEE: KEEP still receives data; KEEP node stays active (not closed)
5) FAIL (old bug): KEEP goes closed / Debug stops when DISABLE ME is disabled
6) Optional: re-enable DISABLE ME, or delete it — KEEP must still run

Demo TCP port: 10517

Related: docs/p4nr/capabilities/shared-client-deregister-isolation.md
Consumers on a *different* Client are unrelated — this tab uses one shared client on purpose.

Config nodes are flow-scoped: delete this tab to remove client/IO config.

name: FC3 qty 2
func:
msg.payload = { fc: 3, unitid: 1, address: 0, quantity: 2 };
return msg;

name: FC3 qty 4
